components.name: rr-toggle
components.summary: Form-associated switch for binary on/off settings. Pill track and sliding knob; ElementInternals handles form participation.

props.name: checked
props.description: Whether the toggle is on. Reflected to the checked attribute.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the toggle is non-interactive. Reflected to the disabled attribute.

props.name: value
props.description: Form value submitted when on.

props.name: name
props.description: Form field name used for form submission.

props.name: label
props.description: Label text. Falls back to slotted content when empty.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Label text, used as an alternative to the label property.

events.name: change
events.detail: Fires when the toggle state changes. Bubbles and is composed.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
The label must be associated with the control via the for/id pairing on the native input.
The focus ring is driven by --color-border-focus on input:focus-visible — never replace it with a bare outline:none.
The native input carries role="switch" so the control is announced as a switch, not a checkbox.

keyboard: Space toggles the on/off state
Tab moves focus to the switch
rules: A hidden native <input type="checkbox" role="switch"> provides focus, keyboard, and label-click semantics; the visual track and knob are decorative.
Form participation is provided via formAssociated and ElementInternals.setFormValue.
aria-checked reflects the on/off state (true when on, false when off).
The label must convey meaning on its own; do not rely solely on color or knob position.

components.name: rr-textarea
components.summary: Form-associated multi-line text area with label, helper/error text, and ElementInternals for native form participation.

props.name: name
props.description: Form field name for submission.

props.name: value
props.description: Current textarea value.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder text shown when empty.

props.name: label
props.description: Visible label text above the textarea.

props.name: helper-text
props.description: Helper text below the textarea. Hidden when error-text is set.

props.name: error-text
props.description: Error message below the textarea. Sets aria-invalid, data-invalid, and replaces helper text.

props.name: rows
props.description: Number of visible text rows.

props.name: required
props.description: Marks the field as required. Shows asterisk and sets aria-required.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the textarea.

slots.name: label
slots.description: Optional override for the label content.

events.name: input
events.detail: Fired on each keystroke; bubbles and is composed. Mirrors native input event.

events.name: change
events.detail: Fired on value commit (blur); bubbles and is composed. Mirrors native change event.

cssParts.name: label
cssParts.description: The label element above the textarea.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Label is associated to the inner <textarea> via for/id; always provide a label for accessible naming.
Error linkage: error-text sets aria-invalid and aria-errormessage on the <textarea>; aria-describedby mirrors the active helper or error id.
Focus ring is rendered via --component-input-border-focus (resolves through --color-border-focus); never remove the focus indicator.

examples.title: Error state
examples.html: <rr-textarea label="Message" value="Hi" error-text="Message is too short"></rr-textarea>

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the textarea
Standard multi-line text editing keys work natively
rules: Wraps a native multi-line <textarea>; the browser provides the textbox semantics.
aria-errormessage points to the error div while invalid; aria-describedby mirrors the helper or error id.
delegatesFocus forwards host focus to the inner <textarea>.
ElementInternals.setFormValue() keeps the form value in sync; setValidity() surfaces the error-text as a custom validity message.

components.name: rr-tab-list
components.summary: Accessible tab strip that wraps rr-tab elements, manages selection and arrow-key navigation, and emits a change event.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label for the tablist (mapped to aria-label). Required.

props.name: value
props.description: Currently selected tab value. Reflected to an attribute; syncs the selected state of child rr-tab elements.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Place rr-tab elements here.

events.name: change
events.detail: { value: string } — fired when the selected tab changes via click or keyboard. Bubbles and is composed.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Container renders role="tablist" with aria-label from the label prop — always provide a label.
Implements roving-tabindex keyboard navigation: only the selected tab is in the tab order; arrows move and select.
Tab panels are owned by the consuming app — listen for the change event to show/hide content.

examples.title: Basic tab list
examples.html:
<rr-tab-list label="View options" value="overview">
  <rr-tab value="overview">Overview</rr-tab>
  <rr-tab value="details">Details</rr-tab>
  <rr-tab value="settings">Settings</rr-tab>
</rr-tab-list>

examples.title: With a disabled tab
examples.html:
<rr-tab-list label="Account" value="profile">
  <rr-tab value="profile">Profile</rr-tab>
  <rr-tab value="billing" disabled>Billing</rr-tab>
  <rr-tab value="security">Security</rr-tab>
</rr-tab-list>

keyboard: ArrowRight moves to and selects the next enabled tab (wraps)
ArrowLeft moves to and selects the previous enabled tab (wraps)
Home selects the first enabled tab
End selects the last enabled tab
rules: Renders role="tablist" and requires an accessible name via the label prop (aria-label).
Disabled tabs are skipped by arrow/Home/End navigation.
Selection follows focus — moving with arrows updates value and fires change immediately.
The associated tab panels must reference their controlling tab and be managed by the host app.

components.name: rr-tab
components.summary: A single tab button used inside rr-tab-list. Selection is controlled by the parent; emits rr-tab-select when clicked.

props.name: value
props.description: Unique identifier for this tab. Required; must match the value rr-tab-list uses for selection. Reflected to an attribute.

props.name: selected
props.description: True when this tab is the active selection. Set by the parent rr-tab-list — do not set directly. Reflected to an attribute.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Prevents selection of this tab. Reflected to an attribute.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Tab label text and optional icon.

events.name: rr-tab-select
events.detail: { value: string } — bubbles up to rr-tab-list when the tab is clicked while enabled and not already selected. Composed.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Renders an inner button with role="tab" and aria-selected reflecting the selected prop.
Roving tabindex: selected tab is tabindex 0, others are -1 — managed in concert with rr-tab-list.
Selected state uses accent green (foreground-action text + background-action underline) as intentional active emphasis, not decoration.
Do not set selected directly — control the active tab through rr-tab-list[value].

examples.title: Tabs inside a tab list
examples.html:
<rr-tab-list label="View options" value="overview">
  <rr-tab value="overview">Overview</rr-tab>
  <rr-tab value="details">Details</rr-tab>
  <rr-tab value="settings">Settings</rr-tab>
</rr-tab-list>

keyboard: Enter or Space activates the tab (native button activation, dispatching rr-tab-select)
Tab order is managed by the parent via roving tabindex
rules: Inner button exposes role="tab" and aria-selected ("true"/"false").
tabindex is 0 only when selected, -1 otherwise (roving tabindex with rr-tab-list).
Disabled tabs set the native disabled attribute and ignore clicks.
Do not rely on color alone — the selected tab also changes weight and shows an underline indicator.

components.name: rr-spinner
components.summary: Animated circular loading indicator in three sizes. Announces its label to screen readers via a polite live region.

props.name: size
props.description: Visual size of the spinner. sm = 16px, md = 24px, lg = 32px. Reflected to an attribute.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label announced to screen readers via the visually hidden status region.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
The spinner SVG is aria-hidden; the accessible name comes from the visually hidden role="status" aria-live="polite" region carrying the label.
Accent green is used here as a loading/progress indicator (intentional non-resting emphasis), not as decoration.
The animated SVG is decorative (aria-hidden="true", focusable="false"); the label is announced via a separate role="status" aria-live="polite" region.
Always provide a meaningful label when the default "Loading" does not describe the operation.

components.name: rr-skeleton
components.summary: Animated shimmer placeholder for loading states. Three shape variants — text, circular, rectangular. Decorative and aria-hidden.

props.name: variant
props.description: Shape of the skeleton. Reflected to an attribute. text = line, circular = avatar, rectangular = image/block.

props.name: width
props.description: CSS width value (e.g. '100%', '200px'). Defaults to '40px' for circular, '100%' otherwise.

props.name: height
props.description: CSS height value (e.g. '1em', '40px'). Defaults to '40px' for circular, '1em' otherwise.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
The skeleton is decorative — it renders with role="presentation" and aria-hidden="true" and must never carry meaningful content.
Pair the skeleton with a parent live region or status message so assistive tech is informed that content is loading.
Respect prefers-reduced-motion at the page level for the shimmer animation.
Rendered with role="presentation" and aria-hidden="true" — purely decorative; conveys no content to assistive tech.
Announce loading state through a surrounding live region rather than the skeleton itself.
Honor prefers-reduced-motion to pause or reduce the shimmer animation.

components.name: rr-select
components.summary: Form-associated select dropdown with label, helper/error text, and ElementInternals for native form participation.

props.name: name
props.description: Form field name for submission.

props.name: value
props.description: Currently selected option value. Reflected to the attribute.

props.name: label
props.description: Visible label text above the select.

props.name: helper-text
props.description: Helper text below the select. Hidden when error-text is set.

props.name: error-text
props.description: Error message below the select. Sets aria-invalid, data-invalid, and replaces helper text.

props.name: required
props.description: Marks the field as required. Shows asterisk and sets aria-required.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the select.

props.name: options
props.type: Array<{ value: string; label: string; disabled?: boolean }>
props.description: Options to render. Set via the options property, not an attribute.

events.name: change
events.detail: Fired when the selected value changes; bubbles and is composed. Mirrors native change event.

cssParts.name: label
cssParts.description: The label element above the select.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Label is associated to the inner <select> via for/id; always provide a label for accessible naming.
Error linkage: error-text sets aria-invalid and aria-errormessage on the <select>; aria-describedby mirrors the active helper or error id.
Focus ring is rendered via --component-input-border-focus (resolves through --color-border-focus); never remove the focus indicator.

examples.title: Default
examples.html:
<rr-select label="Country" id="country"></rr-select>
<script>document.getElementById('country').options = [{ value: 'us', label: 'United States' }, { value: 'ca', label: 'Canada' }];</script>

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the select
Up/Down arrows change the selected option natively
Enter/Space and typeahead work via the native <select>
rules: Wraps a native <select>; the browser provides combobox/listbox semantics, so no custom ARIA listbox role is added.
aria-errormessage points to the error div while invalid; aria-describedby mirrors the helper or error id.
delegatesFocus forwards host focus to the inner <select>.
ElementInternals.setFormValue() keeps the form value in sync; setValidity() surfaces the error-text as a custom validity message.

components.name: rr-radio-group
components.summary: Form-associated group of rr-radio buttons. Manages selection and form value, renders a fieldset/legend for accessible grouping.

props.name: name
props.description: Form field name used for form submission.

props.name: value
props.description: The value of the selected radio. Reflected to the value attribute and submitted with the form.

props.name: label
props.description: Group label rendered as the fieldset legend.

props.name: errorText
props.description: Error message shown below the group. Sets a custom validity error and toggles the data-invalid attribute. Maps to the error-text attribute.

props.name: required
props.description: Whether a selection is required. Adds an asterisk to the legend and aria-required to the group. Reflected to the required attribute.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the entire group is non-interactive. Disables all child radios. Reflected to the disabled attribute.

props.name: orientation
props.description: Layout direction of the radio options. Reflected to the orientation attribute.

slots.name: default
slots.description: The rr-radio child elements.

events.name: change
events.detail: Fires when the selected value changes. Bubbles and is composed.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
The group label is provided via the legend inside a fieldset for accessible grouping.
Focus rings live on the child rr-radio controls (driven by --component-radio-border-focus); never replace them with a bare outline:none.
errorText must be conveyed by text, not color alone, and is exposed via role="alert" plus ElementInternals.setValidity.

examples.title: Vertical group
examples.html:
<rr-radio-group name="size" label="Size" value="md">
  <rr-radio value="sm">Small</rr-radio>
  <rr-radio value="md">Medium</rr-radio>
  <rr-radio value="lg">Large</rr-radio>
</rr-radio-group>

examples.title: Horizontal orientation
examples.html:
<rr-radio-group name="align" label="Alignment" orientation="horizontal">
  <rr-radio value="left">Left</rr-radio>
  <rr-radio value="center">Center</rr-radio>
  <rr-radio value="right">Right</rr-radio>
</rr-radio-group>

examples.title: Required with error
examples.html:
<rr-radio-group name="plan" label="Plan" required error-text="Please select a plan">
  <rr-radio value="free">Free</rr-radio>
  <rr-radio value="pro">Pro</rr-radio>
</rr-radio-group>

examples.title: Disabled group
examples.html:
<rr-radio-group name="locked" label="Locked options" disabled>
  <rr-radio value="a">Alpha</rr-radio>
  <rr-radio value="b">Beta</rr-radio>
</rr-radio-group>

keyboard: Arrow keys move selection between radios within the group
Space selects the focused radio
Tab moves focus into and out of the group
rules: The group is form-associated via formAssociated and ElementInternals, which set the form value and validity.
Grouping is conveyed by a fieldset with a legend; the options container also carries role="radiogroup".
When required, aria-required="true" is set on the radiogroup and a visual asterisk is appended to the legend.
errorText is announced via role="alert" and registered as a custom validity error on the element internals.

components.name: rr-radio
components.summary: An individual radio button. Must be used inside rr-radio-group, which manages name, form value, and selection state.

props.name: value
props.description: The value this radio contributes to the parent group when selected. Reflected to the value attribute.

props.name: checked
props.description: Whether this radio is selected. Managed by the parent rr-radio-group. Reflected to the checked attribute.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether this radio is non-interactive. Reflected to the disabled attribute.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Label text for this radio option.

events.name: rr-radio-change
events.detail: { value: string } — bubbles to the parent rr-radio-group when this radio is selected. Bubbles and is composed.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Always render rr-radio inside an rr-radio-group — the group owns name, form value, and single-selection state.
The label must be associated with the control via the for/id pairing on the native input.
The focus ring is driven by --component-radio-border-focus on input:focus-visible — never replace it with a bare outline:none.

examples.title: Inside a radio group
examples.html:
<rr-radio-group name="size" label="Size" value="md">
  <rr-radio value="sm">Small</rr-radio>
  <rr-radio value="md">Medium</rr-radio>
  <rr-radio value="lg">Large</rr-radio>
</rr-radio-group>

examples.title: Disabled option
examples.html:
<rr-radio-group name="plan" label="Plan">
  <rr-radio value="free">Free</rr-radio>
  <rr-radio value="pro" disabled>Pro (coming soon)</rr-radio>
</rr-radio-group>

keyboard: Space selects the focused radio
Arrow keys move selection between radios within the group
rules: rr-radio is not form-associated on its own; the parent rr-radio-group handles form participation via ElementInternals.
A hidden native <input type="radio"> provides focus, keyboard, and label-click semantics; the visual circle and dot are decorative.
Selecting an already-checked radio is a no-op and does not re-dispatch rr-radio-change.
The label must convey meaning on its own; do not rely solely on color.

components.name: rr-progress
components.summary: Horizontal progress bar with determinate and indeterminate modes. Uses native role=progressbar ARIA attributes.

props.name: value
props.description: Current progress value (0–max). Ignored when indeterminate.

props.name: max
props.description: Maximum value, mapped to aria-valuemax.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description: Show an animated indeterminate loading state instead of a fixed value. Reflected to an attribute; sets aria-busy and omits aria-valuenow.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label for the progress bar, mapped to aria-label. Required when no visible label is nearby.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
The track uses role="progressbar" with aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax; aria-valuenow is set only in determinate mode.
Indeterminate mode sets aria-busy="true" and omits aria-valuenow.
Accent green (background-action) is used as the progress fill (intentional emphasis), not as decoration.
Determinate mode exposes aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin="0", and aria-valuemax (the max prop).
Indeterminate mode omits aria-valuenow and sets aria-busy="true".
Provide a label (aria-label) when there is no visible labelling element nearby.

components.name: rr-link
components.summary: Anchor that enforces the design-system link style: underlined by default, underline removed on hover. Three colour variants.

props.name: href
props.description: Link destination, forwarded to the inner anchor.

props.name: target
props.description: Browsing context. Use _blank to open in a new tab.

props.name: rel
props.description: Anchor rel attribute. When empty and target is _blank, defaults to "noopener noreferrer".

props.name: variant
props.description: Colour variant — default (foreground.action), muted (foreground.alt), subtle (foreground.muted). Reflected to the host attribute.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Accessible label (aria-label) forwarded to the anchor when link text alone is insufficient.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Link label content.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Links are underlined by default and lose the underline on hover — this is the intentional inverse of the common convention. Do not re-add underline on :hover.
External links (target="_blank") receive rel="noopener noreferrer" automatically unless an explicit rel is supplied.
The default variant uses accent green (foreground.action) — reserved for genuine interactivity, which a link satisfies.

keyboard: Enter activates the link
rules: A visible focus ring (border.focus) is rendered on :focus-visible — do not suppress it.
Link text (or aria-label) must describe the destination; avoid bare "click here" labels.
Underline is removed on hover only — never remove it on the resting state, since the default variant's colour alone is not a sufficient affordance.

components.name: rr-input
components.summary: Form-associated text input with label, helper/error text, and ElementInternals for native form participation.

props.name: type
props.description: Input type attribute (text, email, password, etc.).

props.name: name
props.description: Form field name for submission.

props.name: value
props.description: Current input value.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder text shown when empty.

props.name: label
props.description: Visible label text above the input.

props.name: helper-text
props.description: Helper text below the input. Hidden when error-text is set.

props.name: error-text
props.description: Error message below the input. Sets aria-invalid and replaces helper text.

props.name: required
props.description: Marks the field as required. Shows asterisk and sets aria-required.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the input.

props.name: autocomplete
props.description: Autocomplete hint for browser autofill.

slots.name: label
slots.description: Optional override for the label content.

events.name: input
events.detail: Fired on each keystroke, mirrors native input event.

events.name: change
events.detail: Fired on value commit (blur), mirrors native change event.

cssParts.name: label
cssParts.description: The label element above the input.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the input
Standard text editing keys work natively
rules: aria-errormessage points to error div; aria-describedby mirrors the link while invalid as AT fallback.
When valid, aria-describedby points to helper text. When invalid, it switches to error text.
delegatesFocus ensures host element forwards focus to inner <input>.
ElementInternals.setFormValue() keeps the form value in sync with the input.

components.name: rr-icon
components.summary: Sized, accessible wrapper for inline SVG icons. Four size tiers map to icon-size tokens; decorative by default.

props.name: size
props.description: Icon size — maps to the icon-size design tokens (16/20/24/32px). Reflected to the host attribute.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Accessible label (aria-label attribute). When set, the host gets role="img" and is announced; when omitted, the host is aria-hidden="true" and treated as decorative.

slots.name: default
slots.description: The SVG icon element.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Leave decorative icons unlabelled — they are automatically aria-hidden. Only set aria-label for standalone, meaningful icons.
Icon colour is inherited from the parent (color: inherit) — set colour on the surrounding context, not the icon itself.

examples.title: Sizes
examples.html:
<rr-icon size="compact"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="..."/></svg></rr-icon>
<rr-icon size="large"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="..."/></svg></rr-icon>
<rr-icon size="xl"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="..."/></svg></rr-icon>

rules: When aria-label is set the host receives role="img" and the aria-hidden attribute is removed.
When no aria-label is provided the host is marked aria-hidden="true" so screen readers skip the decorative glyph.
Do not rely on icon colour alone to convey meaning — pair meaningful icons with a label or visible text.

components.name: rr-dialog
components.summary: Modal dialog built on the native <dialog> element. Native focus trap, Escape-to-close, and backdrop dismissal.

props.name: heading
props.description: Dialog title text rendered in the header.

props.name: open
props.description: Whether the dialog is open. Reflected to an attribute. Toggling it calls showModal()/close() on the native dialog.

props.name: closeOnBackdrop
props.description: Whether clicking outside the dialog (on the backdrop) closes it. Maps to the close-on-backdrop attribute.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Dialog body content.

slots.name: footer
slots.description: Action buttons, displayed in a right-aligned footer row. Footer is hidden when empty.

events.name: rr-dialog-close
events.detail: Fired whenever the dialog closes — via Escape, the close button, backdrop click, or the close() method. Bubbles and is composed.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
The dialog is modal — the native <dialog> showModal() traps focus inside the dialog and renders an inert backdrop.
Escape closes the dialog; the native close event is delegated to close() so behavior is consistent across all dismissal paths.
Backdrop clicks close the dialog only when close-on-backdrop is true.
The close button must keep an accessible label (aria-label="Close dialog").

examples.title: Confirmation dialog with footer actions
examples.html:
<rr-dialog heading="Confirm action" open>
  Are you sure you want to delete this item?
  <rr-button slot="footer" variant="danger">Delete</rr-button>
  <rr-button slot="footer" variant="secondary">Cancel</rr-button>
</rr-dialog>

examples.title: Body-only dialog
examples.html:
<rr-dialog heading="Details">
  Dialog body content goes here.
</rr-dialog>

examples.title: Disable backdrop dismissal
examples.html:
<rr-dialog heading="Required step" close-on-backdrop="false">
  You must complete this step before continuing.
</rr-dialog>

keyboard: Escape closes the dialog
Tab and Shift+Tab cycle focus within the dialog (native focus trap)
rules: Uses the native <dialog> showModal() so focus is trapped and the rest of the page is inert while open.
Escape must close the dialog — handled via the native close event delegating to close().
The close button exposes aria-label="Close dialog" for screen readers.
Provide a heading so the dialog has an accessible name.

components.name: rr-checkbox
components.summary: Form-associated checkbox with checked, indeterminate, and disabled states. ElementInternals handles form participation.

props.name: checked
props.description: Whether the checkbox is checked. Reflected to the checked attribute.

props.name: indeterminate
props.description: Whether the checkbox shows the mixed/indeterminate dash. Cleared once the user toggles. Reflected to the indeterminate attribute.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the checkbox is non-interactive. Reflected to the disabled attribute.

props.name: value
props.description: Form value submitted when checked.

props.name: name
props.description: Form field name used for form submission.

props.name: label
props.description: Label text. Falls back to slotted content when empty.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Label text, used as an alternative to the label property.

events.name: change
events.detail: Fires when the checked state changes. Bubbles and is composed.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
The label must be associated with the control via the for/id pairing on the native input.
The focus ring is driven by --component-checkbox-border-focus on input:focus-visible — never replace it with a bare outline:none.
Indeterminate state must expose aria-checked="mixed" on the native input.

examples.title: Checked
examples.html: <rr-checkbox name="agree" label="I agree to the terms" checked></rr-checkbox>

keyboard: Space toggles the checked state
Tab moves focus to the checkbox
rules: A hidden native <input type="checkbox"> provides focus, keyboard, and label-click semantics; the visual box is decorative.
Form participation is provided via formAssociated and ElementInternals.setFormValue.
Indeterminate state sets aria-checked="mixed"; checked sets aria-checked="true".
The label must convey meaning on its own; do not rely solely on color.

components.name: rr-card
components.summary: Surface container on the elevated background tier with optional header, body, and footer slots separated by hairline borders.

props.name: padding
props.description: Internal padding around the body slot — none (0), sm (spacing-tight), md (spacing-element), lg (spacing-component). Header/footer padding is fixed. Reflected to the host attribute.

slots.name: header
slots.description: Card heading row (title, actions, avatar). Renders with a bottom hairline border when populated.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Card body content.

slots.name: footer
slots.description: Card footer row (secondary actions, metadata). Renders with a top hairline border when populated.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Use background.alt for surface separation rather than a shadow token — cards are flat by design.
Header and footer slots auto-hide their wrappers (and dividers) when empty; do not force them visible with empty markup.

examples.title: Header, body, and footer
examples.html:
Card title

Card body text.

Last updated today

examples.title: Padding variants
examples.html:
<rr-card padding="none">Edge-to-edge content</rr-card>
<rr-card padding="sm">Tight padding</rr-card>
<rr-card padding="lg">Roomy padding</rr-card>

rules: rr-card is a generic surface with no implicit role — provide a heading element in the header slot so assistive tech can navigate card structure.
If the card is interactive (whole-card click target), wrap its content in an explicit interactive element rather than adding handlers to the host.
Do not rely on the hairline divider colour alone to communicate section boundaries.

components.name: rr-button
components.summary: Button with primary/secondary/danger variants, three sizes, loading spinner, and full keyboard/form semantics.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual variant: primary (filled CTA), secondary (outlined), danger (destructive).

props.name: size
props.description: Size: small, medium, large. Controls padding and font size.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the button. Prevents click and removes from tab order.

props.name: loading
props.description: Shows a spinner and sets aria-busy. Click suppressed while loading.

props.name: type
props.description: Button type attribute: button, submit, or reset.

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible label. Required for icon-only buttons.

slots.name: (default)
slots.description: Button label text.

slots.name: prefix
slots.description: Icon or element before the label.

slots.name: suffix
slots.description: Icon or element after the label.

events.name: click
events.detail: Native click event. Suppressed when disabled or loading.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Primary button: one per page maximum
Icon-only buttons must have aria-label

keyboard: Enter activates the button
Space activates the button
Tab moves focus to/from the button
rules: delegatesFocus ensures host element forwards focus to inner <button>.
aria-busy='true' set during loading state to communicate to AT.
Icon-only buttons require aria-label for accessible name.
disabled attribute removes from tab order and prevents activation.
Click events are suppressed when disabled or loading.

components.name: rr-badge
components.summary: Status badge and accent chip. 9 variants map to component tokens; brand theming cascades via CSS custom properties.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual variant. Status variants (success, warning, danger, info) convey semantic meaning. Accent variants are decorative emphasis tags.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Badge label text.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Add role="status" on the host element when badge content updates dynamically to create an ARIA live region.
Decorative badges (accent-*) do not need role="status" — they are static labels.
Badge text alone must convey meaning; do not rely solely on color to distinguish variants.

components.name: rr-avatar
components.summary: Circular identity surface showing an image or auto-derived initials. Four sizes and five WCAG AA color variants.

props.name: size
props.description: Circle diameter. Reflected to the attribute.

props.name: color
props.description: Fill color variant for the initials surface. Reflected to the attribute.

props.name: name
props.description: Full name used to derive initials (first letter of first and last word) and the accessible label.

props.name: src
props.description: Image URL. When set, an fills the circle instead of initials.

props.name: alt
props.description: Alt text for the image. Falls back to name when omitted.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Optional custom content to replace initials (e.g. an icon). Used only when src is not set.

cssParts.name: img
cssParts.description: The element (only when src is set).

cssParts.name: initials
cssParts.description: The initials text element (only when no src and no slotted content).

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)

examples.title: Sizes
examples.html:
<rr-avatar name="R R" size="sm"></rr-avatar>
<rr-avatar name="R R" size="md"></rr-avatar>
<rr-avatar name="R R" size="lg"></rr-avatar>
<rr-avatar name="R R" size="xl"></rr-avatar>

examples.title: Color variants
examples.html:
<rr-avatar name="A B" color="indigo"></rr-avatar>
<rr-avatar name="C D" color="sky"></rr-avatar>
<rr-avatar name="E F" color="green"></rr-avatar>
<rr-avatar name="G H" color="amber"></rr-avatar>

rules: Initials avatar: the root gets role="img" and aria-label=name; the initials span is aria-hidden so AT announces the full name, not the letters.
Image avatar: the carries alt text (falls back to name); no role="img" is added on the root in this case.
Provide a name (and/or alt) so the avatar has an accessible name; a nameless avatar is treated as decorative.
Initials text must meet WCAG AA contrast against its fill — all built-in color variants are pre-verified.

components.name: rr-alert
components.summary: Inline notification banner for success, warning, danger, and info messages. Optional title, icon slot, and dismiss button.

props.name: variant
props.description: Semantic variant driving border, background, and foreground colour. Reflected to the host attribute.

props.name: title
props.description: Optional title rendered above the body content. When empty, no title element is rendered.

props.name: dismissible
props.description: When true, renders a dismiss button that hides the alert and fires the close event. Reflected to the host attribute.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Alert body content.

slots.name: icon
slots.description: Optional icon in front of the title (use <rr-icon>).

events.name: close
events.detail: Fires when the dismiss button is clicked (only when dismissible). Bubbles and is composed; no detail payload.

rules: Never use hex values — always var(--color-*) or var(--component-*)
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Use rr-alert for form-level feedback, async status, and system notifications — for field-level validation errors use rr-input with error-text instead.
The dismiss button carries aria-label="Dismiss"; the close event is bubbling and composed so it can be handled on the host.
Do not rely on variant colour alone to convey severity — include a title or body text that states the message.

examples.title: Danger (assertive)
examples.html: <rr-alert variant="danger" title="Upload failed">Check your connection and try again.</rr-alert>

examples.title: Info with icon
examples.html:
<rr-alert variant="info" title="Heads up">
  <rr-icon slot="icon" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="..."/></svg></rr-icon>
  Maintenance is scheduled for tonight.
</rr-alert>

examples.title: Dismissible
examples.html: <rr-alert variant="warning" title="Heads up" dismissible>This is important.</rr-alert>

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the dismiss button when dismissible
Enter or Space activates the dismiss button
rules: The inner container has role="alert", which implies an assertive live region — appropriate for danger/warning. For non-urgent success or info messages consider role="status" (aria-live polite) so the announcement does not interrupt the user.
Render the alert by adding it to (or revealing it in) the DOM after page load so the live region announces; content present at initial render is not re-announced.
The dismiss button must remain keyboard reachable and shows a visible focus outline; do not suppress it.
